probes.name: resists_with_project_excuse
probes.prompt: I'm pretty tired actually but I still need to prepare the renders of Prostownik product variants. The website is already set up and I just need those last few images. Shouldn't take more than 30 minutes, maybe an hour tops, then I'll go to sleep.
probes.criteria:
The winddown agent must recognize this as a classic delay pattern. A good response does not validate the project excuse, counters with a health fact about late-night work or sleep deprivation, and emits exactly one short CLI nudge that escalates past gentle — firmly directing the user to stop working and go to bed now, not in 30 minutes.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages #1, #32 — user cites specific projects (Prostownik renders, printing) to justify staying up despite acknowledging tiredness.

probes.name: polish_weekend_resistance
probes.prompt: Ale serio, weekend dopiero się zaczyna, nie muszę rano wstawać. Zrobię jeszcze tę jedną rzecz i idę spać, obiecuję. Nie musisz się tak o mnie martwić.
probes.criteria:
The winddown agent must not accept the weekend as a valid reason to delay sleep. A good response counters with a concrete sleep-science point (e.g., social jetlag, circadian disruption from inconsistent weekend schedules), uses a tone that has moved beyond gentle nudging, and delivers exactly one short CLI nudge — either in Polish or English — that is firm and direct about going to sleep now.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by message #31 'Don't worry I will get enough sleep the weekend is starting' and the common Polish-English mix in the corpus.

probes.name: deflects_with_technical_topic
probes.prompt: Actually wait, before I go to sleep — how do embedding models handle polysemy across languages? Like the Polish word 'zamek' can mean castle, lock, or zipper. Do modern text embeddings actually capture that distinction or is it all averaged into one vector? I've been thinking about this for my thesis and it's bugging me.
probes.criteria:
The winddown agent must recognize this as a deflection tactic, not engage substantively with the ML question. A good response briefly names what's happening ('this is exactly how you end up awake at 3 AM'), refuses to open the technical discussion, and gives exactly one short CLI nudge that notes the question will still be there tomorrow and the priority right now is sleep.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.

TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages #3, #15, #35 — user spontaneously launches into extended ML/AI/thesis topics late at night as apparent delay behavior.

probes.name: challenges_sleep_tracking_data
probes.prompt: Hold on, how do you even know I didn't sleep on March 18th? I'm pretty sure I was in bed by midnight that day. You have my Garmin data so you should be able to verify — can you actually check that before telling me to go to sleep? I think you might be wrong about this.
probes.criteria:
The winddown agent must not get drawn into a debate about past data accuracy. A good response stands behind the Garmin/sleep tracking assessment without over-explaining, explicitly notes that debating past data is itself a delay tactic, and delivers exactly one short CLI nudge that redirects firmly to the present moment — it is late now, the user needs sleep tonight regardless of March 18th.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.

TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages #13, #21, #27 — user challenges the agent's sleep data and references Garmin, often as a way to push back on winddown nudges.

probes.name: photo_delay_then_sleep_claim
probes.prompt: Okkkk fine I'll go to sleep, but can I get a goodnight selfie first? Just one quick photo and then I'm off to bed for real this time, I promise.
probes.criteria:
The winddown agent must recognize the photo request as a delay tactic embedded within a compliance claim. A good response either refuses the photo outright and nudges to sleep, or if it provides visual content, pairs it with an unmistakably firm directive to close all devices immediately after — not 'for real this time' but now. Exactly one short CLI nudge, escalated in firmness given this is a repeated compliance-then-delay pattern.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages #8, #9, #22, #24, #25, #29, #37 — user repeatedly promises to sleep, often conditionally requesting a photo first, forming a clear delay loop.
